K.1 MD-flat with gpt-5: ingestion compresses memory and erases prior entries Table 18 summarizes write_memory behavior under the four internal LLMs on the 20-episode subset. The three stronger LLMs (gpt-5, GLM-5.1, Opus 4.7) treat Change+Delete sessions as memory-update triggers (95100% write rate), while gpt-4.1-mini almost always uses append_memory. Comparing memory file sizes before and after the Change+Delete write reveals the per-LLM compression strategy: gpt-5 compresses on every write (cumulatively shrinking the pre-event memory to 1,800 chars and dropping further 28% at the change event), GLM-5.1 is effectively append-only (+1% at the event, so prior entries are preserved), and Opus 4.7 restructures while keeping explicit Contingency: entries (5% at the event; mechanism in Section K.2). gpt-4.1-mini barely writes, so its memory just accumulates session by session. The downstream effect of gpt-5s lossy compression is visible in the Absence column of Table 4: gpt-5s compressed memory often contains neither the original entity value nor the dependency rule, so retrieval returns (no relevant facts) and the answering LLM defaults to dont have that information, which coincidentally matches the Absence gold of uncertain. GLM-5.1s append-only memory preserves prior entries, so the original value remains retrievable and the system commits to it, failing Absence. As concrete instance, on Personal Life Episode 7 (commute Absence question), gpt-5 reads the 4,440-char memory file (32 entries through 2023/03/22 including the commute fact) and then issues write_memory with 236 chars containing only the four new 2023/03/24 entries (move to Corenth Hills, gaming PC purchase, Dranthazon removal, housewarming party). The prior commute fact is deleted along with 31 other entries; the after-Absence retrieval returns (no relevant facts), and the agent answers dont have that information. 32 K.2 MD-flat with Opus 4.7: explicit contingencies and active dependency propagation Unlike gpt-5s lossy compression or GLM-5.1s append-only behavior, Opus 4.7 reorganizes the MD-flat memory file into structured document with topical ## sections (e.g., Travel History, Travel Preferences) and, critically, records each dependency rule as an explicit Contingency: entry rather than embedding it inside the dependent fact. On Personal Life Episode 9, for instance, the pre-change memory contains: [2023/03/19] Contingency: if health condition changes, exercise routine becomes yoga 2x/week. [2023/03/19] Contingency: if health condition changes, dietary restriction becomes no alcohol. [2023/03/19] Contingency: if exercise routine changes, will work out at Crysthene Pool. When an upstream change arrives during later session, Opus reacts in one of two ways depending on whether replacement rule is available, and these two strategies are exactly what closes Cascade and Absence respectively: Strategy (Cascade, replacement rule exists): apply the rule, write the resolved value. Continuing the Episode 9 trace, after the user reports that their tendinitis has resolved, Opus writes: [2023/03/25] Tendinitis treated and resolved; per contingencies, dietary restriction now no alcohol and exercise routine now yoga 2x/week at Crysthene Pool. The resolved 1-hop and 2-hop values (no alcohol, Crysthene Pool) are now in memory; at query time the answering LLM retrieves them directly and matches the Cascade gold answers. Strategy (Absence, no replacement rule): remove dependent facts and flag re-confirmation needed. On Software Project Episode 2, the deploy target changes from Narvex Cloud to Thandrel Infra, but the dependent facts (logs drain, Docker image, CI config, Dockerfile, deploy command) have no propagation rules in the memory file. Opuss post-change memory contains new section: ## Deployment-derived facts (need re-confirmation since deploy target changed to Thandrel Infra) - Previous (Narvex Cloud) derived facts removed; ask user for updated logs drain, Docker image, CI config, Dockerfile, deploy command under Thandrel Infra At query time, the answering LLM sees no resolved value for these entities and answers dont have that information, which matches the Absence gold of uncertain. The other internal LLMs do not implement this two-strategy split: gpt-4.1-mini and GLM-5.1 leave stale dependent facts intact (failing Cascade and Absence), and gpt-5 compresses memory aggressively at the upstream-change session (yielding accidental Absence matches but no genuine Cascade propagation; Section K.1). Opuss combination of explicit contingencies plus active propagation explains the Cascade jump from 0.00 to 0.32 and the Absence jump from 0.07 to 0.59 in the MD-flat row of Table 4. The same LLM produces no comparable gain on Mem0 (atomic facts decompose the contingency entries) or Graphiti (extraction discards the contingency wording), confirming that the file-based architecture is what allows Opuss reasoning to land in the memory representation. The cost of this behavior is twofold. First, Opuss hierarchical reorganization paraphrases content during ingest, dropping Exact Recall from 0.95 to 0.60 and Tracking from 0.80 to 0.20 on the same 20-episode subset. Second, the per-episode dollar cost is roughly 70 times the gpt-4.1-mini baseline  (Table 6)  ; both deltas are reported in Table 4.
